label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out why the author wrote the text. Let's start by reading through the text again to get the main idea. The author starts by talking about joining an exchange study program and being hosted by a French couple. They mention learning French, moving in with the couple, and then having to move out due to a family death. Then they moved into a new place with a Brazilian roommate named Paolo, formed a good friendship, and ended up having a great experience. At the end, the author says they recommend the exchange program for experiencing foreign cultures and gaining friendships. Now, the question is asking why the author wrote the text. The options are A through D. Let me look at each option: A) To show the importance of learning foreign cultures. The text does mention learning French and experiencing French/Brazilian culture, but the main focus seems to be on the personal experience rather than emphasizing the importance of learning cultures in general. B) To discuss how to build meaningful friendships. The text does talk about making a friend, Paolo, and the friendship being meaningful, but the author isn't really giving advice or methods on how to build friendships. It's more about their personal story. C) To explain how exchange study programs work. The text describes the author's personal experience in the program but doesn't explain the mechanics or processes of how these programs work. It's more about their specific experience. D) To describe his experiences in an exchange program. This seems to fit because the entire text is a narrative of the author's time in the program, from arrival, host
